[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Men%27s+FIH+Hockey+World+Cup%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Men%27s+FIH+Hockey+World+Cup%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Men%27s+FIH+Hockey+World+Cup%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Men%27s+FIH+Hockey+World+Cup%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(January 2026)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |
Current sports event *[2026 Men's FIH Hockey World Cup](/wiki/2026_Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup "2026 Men's FIH Hockey World Cup")* | |

is an international [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") competition organised by the [International Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation "International Hockey Federation"). The tournament was [started in 1971](/wiki/1971_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1971 Men's Hockey World Cup"). It is held every four years, bridging the four years between the [Summer Olympics](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the Summer Olympics"). [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Pakistan men's national field hockey team") is the most successful team, having won the tournament four times. The [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national field hockey team"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Australia men's national field hockey team"), and [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team") have each won three titles. [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Belgium men's national field hockey team") and [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") have both won the tournament once.

The **Men's FIH Hockey World Cup** is an international [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") competition organised by the [International Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation "International Hockey Federation"). The tournament was [started in 1971](/wiki/1971_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1971 Men's Hockey World Cup"). It is held every four years, bridging the four years between the [Summer Olympics](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the Summer Olympics"). [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Pakistan men's national field hockey team") is the most successful team, having won the tournament four times. The [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national field hockey team"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Australia men's national field hockey team"), and [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team") have each won three titles. [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Belgium men's national field hockey team") and [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") have both won the tournament once.

The [2023 tournament](/wiki/2023_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "2023 Men's Hockey World Cup") was held in [Bhubaneswar](/wiki/Bhubaneswar "Bhubaneswar") and [Rourkela](/wiki/Rourkela "Rourkela"), India from 13 to 29 January. [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team") defeated [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Belgium men's national field hockey team") in a penalty shoot-out 5–4 after the match ended in a 3–3 draw to win their third World Cup title. The World Cup expanded to 16 teams in 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Hockey World Cup was first conceived by [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan")'s Air Marshal [Nur Khan](/wiki/Nur_Khan "Nur Khan"). He proposed his idea to the [FIH](/wiki/International_Hockey_Federation "International Hockey Federation") through Patrick Rowley, the first editor of *World Hockey* magazine. Their idea was approved on 26 October 1969 and adopted by the FIH Council at a meeting in Brussels on 12 April 1970. The FIH decided that the inaugural World Cup would be held in October 1971, in Pakistan.

However, political issues would prevent that first competition from being played in Pakistan. The FIH had inadvertently scheduled the first World Cup to be played in Pakistan during the [Bangladesh Liberation War](/wiki/Bangladesh_Liberation_War "Bangladesh Liberation War"). Furthermore, Pakistan and India had been at war with each other only six years earlier. When Pakistan invited India to compete in the tournament, a crisis arose. Pakistanis, led by cricketer [Abdul Hafeez Kardar](/wiki/Abdul_Hafeez_Kardar "Abdul Hafeez Kardar"), protested against India's participation in the Hockey World Cup.

Given the intense political climate between Pakistan and India, the FIH decided to move the tournament elsewhere. In March 1971, coincidentally in the same month [Bangladesh declared independence from Pakistan](/wiki/Bangladeshi_Declaration_of_Independence "Bangladeshi Declaration of Independence"), the FIH decided to move the first Hockey World Cup to the [Real Club de Polo](/wiki/Real_Club_de_Polo "Real Club de Polo") grounds in [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"), [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), which was considered a neutral and peaceful European site.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The FIH has set no requirements or limitations on the size of the competition. The 1971 Cup included only ten nations, the smallest World Cup. The 1978 Cup featured fourteen nations. The 2002, 2018 and 2023 Cups featured sixteen nations. The remaining 10 World Cups have featured 12 nations.

The first three tournaments were held every two years. The 1978 Cup was the only tournament held three years from the previous one. It was halfway between the [Summer Olympics](/wiki/Field_Hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Field Hockey at the Summer Olympics") hockey competition and has continued that way. In other words, the tournament has been held every four years ever since.

## Trophy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Trophy")]

The Hockey World Cup trophy was designed by the Bashir Moojid and created by the [Pakistani Army](/wiki/Pakistan_Army "Pakistan Army"). On 27 March 1971, in [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), the trophy was formally handed to FIH President Rene Frank by Mr H.E Masood, the Pakistani Ambassador to Belgium. The trophy consists of a [silver](/wiki/Silver "Silver") cup with an intricate floral design, surmounted by a globe of the world in silver and [gold](/wiki/Gold "Gold"), placed on a high blade base inlaid with [ivory](/wiki/Ivory "Ivory"). At its peak is a model hockey [stick](/wiki/Hockey_stick "Hockey stick") and ball. Without its base, the trophy stands 120.85 mm (4.758 in) high. Including the base, the trophy stands 650 mm (26 in). It weighs 11,560 [g](/wiki/Gram "Gram") (408 oz), including 895 g (31.6 oz) of gold, 6,815 g (240.4 oz) of silver, 350 g (12 oz) of ivory and 3,500 g (120 oz) of [teak](/wiki/Teak "Teak").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Format")]

The Hockey World Cup consists of a qualification stage and a final tournament stage. The format for each stage is the same.

### Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Qualification")]

The qualification stage has been a part of the Hockey World Cup since 1977. All participating teams play in the qualification round. The teams divide into two or more pools and compete for a berth in the final tournament. The top two teams are automatically qualified and the rest of the berths are decided in playoffs.

### Final tournament

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Final tournament")]

The final tournament features the continental champions and other qualified teams. Sometimes it also features the winners of the Summer Olympics' hockey competition or the continental runners-up. The teams divide into pools once more and play a [round robin tournament](/wiki/Round_robin_tournament "Round robin tournament"). The composition of the pools is determined using the current [FIH World Rankings](/wiki/FIH_World_Rankings "FIH World Rankings").

### Successful national teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Successful national teams")]

Twenty seven teams have qualified for a Hockey World Cup. Of these, thirteen teams have made it to the semifinals. Eight teams have made it through to the finals.

To date, the most successful teams are Pakistan, with four titles from six final appearances, the Netherlands, with three titles from seven final appearances, Germany and Australia with three titles from five final appearances, while India and Belgium won their lone titles in 1975 and 2018, respectively.

### Performance by host nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Performance by host nations")]

Nine nations have hosted the Hockey World Cup. Only the Netherlands ([1973](/wiki/1973_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1973 Men's Hockey World Cup") and [1998](/wiki/1998_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1998 Men's Hockey World Cup")) and Germany ([2006](/wiki/2006_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "2006 Men's Hockey World Cup")) have won the tournament as hosts. Spain, England, and Pakistan emerged as host runners-up in the [1971](/wiki/1971_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1971 Men's Hockey World Cup"), [1986](/wiki/1986_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1986 Men's Hockey World Cup") and [1990](/wiki/1990_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1990 Men's Hockey World Cup") tournaments. Australia placed third when it hosted the [1994](/wiki/1994_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1994 Men's Hockey World Cup") tournament in [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney").

### Performance by continental zones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Men%27s_FIH_Hockey_World_Cup&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Performance by continental zones")]

To date, the finals of the Hockey World Cup have been contested by [Asian](/wiki/Asian_Hockey_Federation "Asian Hockey Federation"), [European](/wiki/European_Hockey_Federation "European Hockey Federation") and [Oceania](/wiki/Oceania_Hockey_Federation "Oceania Hockey Federation") continental teams. European teams have won the most with six titles, followed by Asian teams with five titles. Australia is the only team from Oceania to win the tournament. Neither [the Americas](/wiki/Pan_American_Hockey_Federation "Pan American Hockey Federation") nor [Africa](/wiki/African_Hockey_Federation "African Hockey Federation") have ever won the title.

| Continent | Best performance |
| --- | --- |
| [Europe](/wiki/European_Hockey_Federation "European Hockey Federation") | 7 titles, won by the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national field hockey team") (3), [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team") (3) and [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Belgium men's national field hockey team") (1) |
| [Asia](/wiki/Asian_Hockey_Federation "Asian Hockey Federation") | 5 titles, won by [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Pakistan men's national field hockey team") (4) and [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") (1) |
| [Oceania](/wiki/Oceania_Hockey_Federation "Oceania Hockey Federation") | 3 titles, won by [Australia](/wiki/Kookaburras_(hockey) "Kookaburras (hockey)") |
| [Americas](/wiki/Pan_American_Hockey_Federation "Pan American Hockey Federation") | Third place ([Argentina](/wiki/Argentina_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Argentina men's national field hockey team"), [2014](/wiki/2014_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "2014 Men's Hockey World Cup")) |
| [Africa](/wiki/African_Hockey_Federation "African Hockey Federation") | Fourth place ([Kenya](/wiki/Kenya_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Kenya men's national field hockey team"), [1971](/wiki/1971_Men%27s_Hockey_World_Cup "1971 Men's Hockey World Cup")) |
